chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ وَ مَا لَا يَنْقُضُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – What breaks the Ablution (Wudhu) and what does not break it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/116)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/116)
text: لَيْسَ فِي حَبِّ الْقَرْعِ وَ الدِّيدَانَ الصِّغَارِ وُضُوءٌ إِنَّمَا هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْقَمْلِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad,f rom Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Zareyf, from Sa’alba Bin Maymoun, from Abdullah bin Yazeed,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is not, with regards to a pumpkin seed and the small insects, an Ablution (Wudhu). But rather, it is at the status of the lice’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il from Zarif from Tha’labah ibn Maymun from ‘Abd Allah ibn Yazid who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not necessary to take Wudu’ because of the coming out of one’s buttock, such things as pumpkin seeds or small worms; they are like lice.’”
